tools.name: BEACON
tools.evidence_snippet: BEACON remains the most frequently observed malware family in Mandiant investigations globally and was identified in 10% of all intrusions

tools.name: ALPHV
tools.evidence_snippet: ALPHV ransomware in 5% of Mandiant led investigations in 2023 compared to 2% in 2022

tools.name: LEMURLOOT
tools.evidence_snippet: LEMURLOOT is a web shell written in C# tailored to interact with the MOVEit Transfer platform

tools.name: SYSTEMBC
tools.evidence_snippet: SYSTEMBC is a tunneler written in C that retrieves proxy-related commands from a C2 server

tools.name: LOCKBIT
tools.evidence_snippet: LOCKBIT ransomware appeared in 4% of investigations compared to 2% in 2022

tools.name: SEASPY
tools.evidence_snippet: SEASPY is a backdoor that establishes a PCAP filter on port 25 (SMTP) and is activated when a magic packet is received

tools.name: METASPLOIT
tools.evidence_snippet: METASPLOIT is a penetration testing framework whose features include vulnerability testing

tools.name: REGEORG
tools.evidence_snippet: REGEORG is an open-source utility used to tunnel webshell traffic

tools.name: SLIVER
tools.evidence_snippet: Sliver was developed as an open-source red team adversary emulation tool

tools.name: FAKEUPDATES
tools.evidence_snippet: Financially Motivated Group Gains Access via Drive-By Downloads of FAKEUPDATES

tools.name: QAKBOT
tools.evidence_snippet: UNC2633 Resurfaces to Distribute QAKBOT via OneNote phishing attachments

tools.name: SMOKELOADER
tools.evidence_snippet: Suspected Russian Group Targets Ukraine with SMOKELOADER

tools.name: PAPERDROP
tools.evidence_snippet: Financially Motivated Group Uses Social Media and SEO Poisoning to Deliver PAPERDROP and DANABOT

cves.evidence_snippet: The most prevalent vulnerability Mandiant investigators observed in 2023 was CVE-2023-34362, an SQL injection vulnerability in MOVEit Transfer
The second most prevalent vulnerability was CVE-2022-21587, a critical unauthenticated file upload vulnerability in Oracle E-Business Suite
The third most prevalent vulnerability in 2023 was CVE-2023-2868, a critical command injection vulnerability in Barracuda Email Security Gateways

Mandiant continues to observe a handful of vulnerabilities related to older technologies, such as Microsoft Access 2003 (CVE-2008-2463), Microsoft Windows Server 2016 (CVE2017-0144), and Telerik (CVE-2019-18935).

Mandiant continues to observe a handful of vulnerabilities related to older technologies, such as Microsoft Access 2003 (CVE-2008-2463), Microsoft Windows Server 2016 (CVE2017-0144), and Telerik (CVE-2019-18935).

Exploitation of CVE-2023-4966 in Citrix Netscaler ADC Appliances

Mandiant also investigated intrusions in which FIN11 exploited CVE-2023-0669 in GoAnywhere MFT

ttps.evidence_snippet: In more than half of investigations, Mandiant investigators noted the use of a command or scripting interpreter (T1059) by attackers
T1543: Create or Modify System Process 28.3%

summary: Mandiant M-Trends 2024 annual report analyzing 2023 global threat landscape. Key findings: global median dwell time decreased to 10 days (from 16 in 2022), 54% external notifications, 46% internal detections. Ransomware investigations increased to 23% (up from 18% in 2022). Initial access: exploits 38% (CVE-2023-34362 MOVEit, CVE-2022-21587 Oracle, CVE-2023-2868 Barracuda), phishing 17%, prior compromise 15%. FIN11 MOVEit campaign (CVE-2023-34362) impacted 2,600+ organizations via LEMURLOOT web shell. UNC4841 Chinese espionage exploited Barracuda ESG (CVE-2023-2868) targeting ASEAN ministries. 97 zero-days exploited in 2023 (56% increase), PRC groups most prolific. BEACON usage declined to 10% despite remaining top malware. 626 new malware families tracked, backdoors 33%, ALPHV/LOCKBIT prominent ransomware. MITRE ATT&CK: 74% techniques, 44% sub-techniques observed; top 5 sub-techniques PowerShell (32.3%), Web Protocols (29.6%), RDP (28.3%), Service Execution (26.8%), File Deletion (26.6%). Regional: Americas 10-day dwell, JAPAC 9-day (fastest), EMEA 22-day. Threat groups: 316 observed (220 newly tracked), APT43 graduated.

timeline.evidence_snippet: The metrics reported in M-Trends 2024 are based on Mandiant Consulting investigations of targeted attack activity conducted between January 1, 2023 and December 31, 2023

timeline.event: FIN11 begins scanning for MOVEit vulnerability
timeline.evidence_snippet: Mandiant investigators observed FIN11 scanning the internet beginning on May 15, 2023
the earliest evidence of exploitation of CVE-2023-34362 occurred twelve days later, on May 27, 2023

timeline.event: Progress Software disclosed CVE-2023-34362 and released patch
timeline.evidence_snippet: On May 31, 2023, Progress disclosed the vulnerability, patched its cloud-based service, and released a patch for on-premises implementations

timeline.event: CVE-2023-34362 added to CISA KEV Catalog
timeline.evidence_snippet: 06-02 CVE-2023-34362 Added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog

timeline.event: FIN11 claimed responsibility on CL0P-LEAKS DLS
timeline.evidence_snippet: on June 6, 2023, that FIN11 claimed responsibility for the campaign on the CL0P-LEAKS DLS

timeline.event: First public PoC for CVE-2023-34362 released
timeline.evidence_snippet: The first PoC was made publicly available three days later, on June 9, 2023
In a December 2023 press release, the United States Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) reported that ALPHV, also known as BlackCat, had targeted more than 1,000 organizations
International law enforcement continued to pursue LOCKBIT activity, announcing seizure of the LOCKBIT data leak site and back-end infrastructure in Operation Cronos in February 2024
